<!--
Read before opening:

ShioriCode is early, maintainer-directed software. We are strict about scope,
quality, and long-term maintenance cost.

Good PRs:
- Fix one clearly described bug.
- Improve reliability, tests, docs, or release tooling.
- Stay small enough to review carefully.
- Match existing architecture instead of adding a parallel system.

PRs we will close:
- Large drive-by rewrites.
- Feature drops without prior maintainer agreement.
- Changes that add dependencies, providers, storage formats, or protocol surface
  without explaining the maintenance cost.
- Cosmetic churn mixed with behavior changes.
- Generated code dumps or broad formatting-only changes.
-->

## What Changed

<!-- Describe the change clearly. Keep the scope tight. -->

## Why

<!-- Explain the bug, risk, or user problem this solves. -->

## Validation

<!-- List the exact checks you ran, for example:
- bun run fmt
- bun run lint
- bun run typecheck
- bun run test
-->

## UI Changes

<!-- If this changes UI, include before/after screenshots.
If this changes motion or interaction, include a short recording.
Write "None" if not applicable. -->

## Risk

<!-- Name migration, compatibility, security, privacy, release, or provider risks.
Write "Low" only if the change is genuinely narrow. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] This PR is small and focused.
- [ ] I explained what changed and why.
- [ ] I ran the relevant validation checks.
- [ ] I included screenshots or a recording for UI changes.
- [ ] I did not mix unrelated refactors with the requested change.
- [ ] I documented new environment variables, commands, or user-visible behavior.
